name: the-politics-industry
description: Katherine M. Gehl and Michael E. Porter's The Politics Industry — a strategic analysis toolkit that applies Porter's Five Forces to the U.S. political system, diagnosing America's political dysfunction as a duopoly problem and prescribing practical innovations to fix it.
useCase: Diagnose the structural causes of political gridlock, understand how the two-party duopoly suppresses competition, and evaluate reforms like Final-Five Voting and ranked-choice voting.
knownRisks: The book focuses on U.S. federal politics — reforms may not apply to other political systems
Political reform is inherently contentious and progress is slow
The Porter Five Forces framework, while useful, is a simplification of complex political dynamics
